gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/34)

parts.text: عِيسَى بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ

text: أَمَرَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) أَنْ يَحُجَّ وَ يُحِجَّ إِسْمَاعِيلَ مَعَهُ وَ يُسْكِنَهُ الْحَرَمَ فَحَجَّا عَلَى جَمَلٍ أَحْمَرَ وَ مَا مَعَهُمَا إِلَّا جَبْرَئِيلُ ( عليه السلام ) فَلَمَّا بَلَغَا الْحَرَمَ قَالَ لَهُ جَبْرَئِيلُ يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ انْزِلَا فَاغْتَسِلَا قَبْلَ أَنْ تَدْخُلَا الْحَرَمَ فَنَزَلَا فَاغْتَسَلَا وَ أَرَاهُمَا كَيْفَ يَتَهَيَّئَانِ لِلْإِحْرَامِ فَفَعَلَا

ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُمَا فَأَهَلَّا بِالْحَجِّ وَ أَمَرَهُمَا بِالتَّلْبِيَاتِ الْأَرْبَعِ الَّتِي لَبَّى بِهَا الْمُرْسَلُونَ ثُمَّ صَارَ بِهِمَا إِلَى الصَّفَا فَنَزَلَا وَ قَامَ جَبْرَئِيلُ بَيْنَهُمَا وَ اسْتَقْبَلَ الْبَيْتَ فَكَبَّرَ اللَّهَ وَ كَبَّرَا وَ هَلَّلَ اللَّهَ وَ هَلَّلَا وَ حَمَّدَ اللَّهَ وَ حَمَّدَا وَ مَجَّدَ اللَّهَ وَ مَجَّدَا وَ أَثْنَى عَلَيْهِ وَ فَعَلَا مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ

وَ تَقَدَّمَ جَبْرَئِيلُ وَ تَقَدَّمَا يُثْنِيَانِ عَلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ يُمَجِّدَانِهِ حَتَّى انْتَهَى بِهِمَا إِلَى مَوْضِعِ الْحَجَرِ فَاسْتَلَمَ جَبْرَئِيلُ الْحَجَرَ وَ أَمَرَهُمَا أَنْ يَسْتَلِمَا وَ طَافَ بِهِمَا أُسْبُوعاً ثُمَّ قَامَ بِهِمَا فِي مَوْضِعِ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَ صَلَّيَا ثُمَّ أَرَاهُمَا الْمَنَاسِكَ وَ مَا يَعْمَلَانِ بِهِ

فَلَمَّا قَضَيَا مَنَاسِكَهُمَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) بِالِانْصِرَافِ وَ أَقَامَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ وَحْدَهُ مَا مَعَهُ أَحَدٌ غَيْرُ أُمِّهِ فَلَمَّا كَانَ مِنْ قَابِلٍ أَذِنَ اللَّهُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الْحَجِّ وَ بِنَاءِ الْكَعْبَةِ وَ كَانَتِ الْعَرَبُ تَحُجُّ إِلَيْهِ وَ إِنَّمَا كَانَ رَدْماً إِلَّا أَنَّ قَوَاعِدَهُ مَعْرُوفَةٌ

فَلَمَّا صَدَرَ النَّاسُ جَمَعَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ الْحِجَارَةَ وَ طَرَحَهَا فِي جَوْفِ الْكَعْبَةِ فَلَمَّا أَذِنَ اللَّهُ لَهُ فِي الْبِنَاءِ قَدِمَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ يَا بُنَيَّ قَدْ أَمَرَنَا اللَّهُ بِبِنَاءِ الْكَعْبَةِ وَ كَشَفَا عَنْهَا فَإِذَا هُوَ حَجَرٌ وَاحِدٌ أَحْمَرُ فَأَوْحَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَيْهِ ضَعْ بِنَاءَهَا عَلَيْهِ وَ أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَرْبَعَةَ أَمْلَاكٍ يَجْمَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ الْحِجَارَةَ

فَكَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ ( عليهما السلام ) يَضَعَانِ الْحِجَارَةَ وَ الْمَلَائِكَةُ تُنَاوِلُهُمَا حَتَّى تَمَّتِ اثْنَا عَشَرَ ذِرَاعاً وَ هَيَّئَا لَهُ بَابَيْنِ بَاباً يُدْخَلُ مِنْهُ وَ بَاباً يُخْرَجُ مِنْهُ وَ وَضَعَا عَلَيْهِ عَتَباً وَ شَرَجاً مِنْ حَدِيدٍ عَلَى أَبْوَابِهِ وَ كَانَتِ الْكَعْبَةُ عُرْيَانَةً فَصَدَرَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ قَدْ سَوَّى الْبَيْتَ وَ أَقَامَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ

فَلَمَّا وَرَدَ عَلَيْهِ النَّاسُ نَظَرَ إِلَى امْرَأَةٍ مِنْ حِمْيَرٍ أَعْجَبَهُ جَمَالُهَا فَسَأَلَ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَنْ يُزَوِّجَهَا إِيَّاهُ وَ كَانَ لَهَا بَعْلٌ فَقَضَى اللَّهُ عَلَى بَعْلِهَا بِالْمَوْتِ وَ أَقَامَتْ بِمَكَّةَ حُزْناً عَلَى بَعْلِهَا فَأَسْلَى اللَّهُ ذَلِكَ عَنْهَا وَ زَوَّجَهَا إِسْمَاعِيلَ

وَ قَدِمَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ الْحَجَّ وَ كَانَتِ امْرَأَةً مُوَفَّقَةً وَ خَرَجَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ إِلَى الطَّائِفِ يَمْتَارُ لِأَهْلِهِ طَعَاماً فَنَظَرَتْ إِلَى شَيْخٍ شَعِثٍ فَسَأَلَهَا عَنْ حَالِهِمْ فَأَخْبَرَتْهُ بِحُسْنِ حَالٍ فَسَأَلَهَا عَنْهُ خَاصَّةً فَأَخْبَرَتْهُ بِحُسْنِ الدِّينِ وَ سَأَلَهَا مِمَّنْ أَنْتِ فَقَالَتْ امْرَأَةٌ مِنْ حِمْيَرٍ فَسَارَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ لَمْ يَلْقَ إِسْمَاعِيلَ وَ قَدْ كَتَبَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ كِتَاباً فَقَالَ ادْفَعِي هَذَا إِلَى بَعْلِكِ إِذَا أَتَى إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ

فَقَدِمَ عَلَيْهَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ فَدَفَعَتْ إِلَيْهِ الْكِتَابَ فَقَرَأَهُ فَقَالَ أَ تَدْرِينَ مَنْ هَذَا الشَّيْخُ فَقَالَتْ لَقَدْ رَأَيْتُهُ جَمِيلًا فِيهِ مُشَابَهَةٌ مِنْكَ قَالَ ذَاكَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ فَقَالَتْ وَا سَوْأَتَاهْ مِنْهُ فَقَالَ وَ لِمَ نَظَرَ إِلَى شَيْ‏ءٍ مِنْ مَحَاسِنِكِ فَقَالَتْ لَا وَ لَكِنْ خِفْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ قَدْ قَصَّرْتُ وَ قَالَتْ لَهُ الْمَرْأَةُ وَ كَانَتْ عَاقِلَةً فَهَلَّا تُعَلِّقُ عَلَى هَذَيْنِ الْبَابَيْنِ سِتْرَيْنِ سِتْراً مِنْ هَاهُنَا وَ سِتْراً مِنْ هَاهُنَا فَقَالَ لَهَا نَعَمْ

فَعَمِلَا لَهُمَا سِتْرَيْنِ طُولُهُمَا اثْنَا عَشَرَ ذِرَاعاً فَعَلَّقَاهُمَا عَلَى الْبَابَيْنِ فَأَعْجَبَهُمَا ذَلِكَ فَقَالَتْ فَهَلَّا أَحُوكُ لِلْكَعْبَةِ ثِيَاباً فَتَسْتُرَهَا كُلَّهَا فَإِنَّ هَذِهِ الْحِجَارَةَ سَمِجَةٌ فَقَالَ لَهَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بَلَى فَأَسْرَعَتْ فِي ذَلِكَ وَ بَعَثَتْ إِلَى قَوْمِهَا بِصُوفٍ كَثِيرٍ تَسْتَغْزِلُهُمْ

قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ إِنَّمَا وَقَعَ اسْتِغْزَالُ النِّسَاءِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ بَعْضِهِنَّ لِبَعْضٍ لِذَلِكَ قَالَ فَأَسْرَعَتْ وَ اسْتَعَانَتْ فِي ذَلِكَ فَكُلَّمَا فَرَغَتْ مِنْ شُقَّةٍ عَلَّقَتْهَا فَجَاءَ الْمَوْسِمُ وَ قَدْ بَقِيَ وَجْهٌ مِنْ وُجُوهِ الْكَعْبَةِ فَقَالَتْ لِإِسْمَاعِيلَ كَيْفَ نَصْنَعُ بِهَذَا الْوَجْهِ الَّذِي لَمْ تُدْرِكْهُ الْكِسْوَةُ فَكَسَوْهُ خَصَفاً

فَجَاءَ الْمَوْسِمُ وَ جَاءَتْهُ الْعَرَبُ عَلَى حَالِ مَا كَانَتْ تَأْتِيهِ فَنَظَرُوا إِلَى أَمْرٍ أَعْجَبَهُمْ فَقَالُوا يَنْبَغِي لِعَامِلِ هَذَا الْبَيْتِ أَنْ يُهْدَى إِلَيْهِ فَمِنْ ثَمَّ وَقَعَ الْهَدْيُ فَأَتَى كُلُّ فَخِذٍ مِنَ الْعَرَبِ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ يَحْمِلُهُ مِنْ وَرِقٍ وَ مِنْ أَشْيَاءَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى اجْتَمَعَ شَيْ‏ءٌ كَثِيرٌ فَنَزَعُوا ذَلِكَ الْخَصَفَ وَ أَتَمُّوا كِسْوَةَ الْبَيْتِ وَ عَلَّقُوا عَلَيْهَا بَابَيْنِ

وَ كَانَتِ الْكَعْبَةُ لَيْسَتْ بِمُسَقَّفَةٍ فَوَضَعَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ فِيهَا أَعْمِدَةً مِثْلَ هَذِهِ الْأَعْمِدَةِ الَّتِي تَرَوْنَ مِنْ خَشَبٍ وَ سَقَّفَهَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بِالْجَرَائِدِ وَ سَوَّاهَا بِالطِّينِ فَجَاءَتِ الْعَرَبُ مِنَ الْحَوْلِ فَدَخَلُوا الْكَعْبَةَ وَ رَأَوْا عِمَارَتَهَا فَقَالُوا يَنْبَغِي لِعَامِلِ هَذَا الْبَيْتِ أَنْ يُزَادَ فَلَمَّا كَانَ مِنْ قَابِلٍ جَاءَهُ الْهَدْيُ فَلَمْ يَدْرِ إِسْمَاعِيلُ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ فَأَوْحَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَيْهِ أَنِ انْحَرْهُ وَ أَطْعِمْهُ الْحَاجَّ

قَالَ وَ شَكَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ إِلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ قِلَّةَ الْمَاءِ فَأَوْحَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ أَنِ احْتَفِرْ بِئْراً يَكُونُ مِنْهَا شَرَابُ الْحَاجِّ فَنَزَلَ جَبْرَئِيلُ ( عليه السلام ) فَاحْتَفَرَ قَلِيبَهُمْ يَعْنِي زَمْزَمَ حَتَّى ظَهَرَ مَاؤُهَا ثُمَّ قَالَ جَبْرَئِيلُ ( عليه السلام ) انْزِلْ يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ فَنَزَلَ بَعْدَ جَبْرَئِيلَ فَقَالَ يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ اضْرِبْ فِي أَرْبَعِ زَوَايَا الْبِئْرِ وَ قُلْ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ

قَالَ فَضَرَبَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الزَّاوِيَةِ الَّتِي تَلِي الْبَيْتَ وَ قَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ فَانْفَجَرَتْ عَيْنٌ ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ فِي الزَّاوِيَةِ الثَّانِيَةِ وَ قَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ فَانْفَجَرَتْ عَيْنٌ ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ فِي الثَّالِثَةِ وَ قَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ فَانْفَجَرَتْ عَيْنٌ ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ فِي الرَّابِعَةِ وَ قَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ فَانْفَجَرَتْ عَيْنٌ

وَ قَالَ لَهُ جَبْرَئِيلُ اشْرَبْ يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ ادْعُ لِوَلَدِكَ فِيهَا بِالْبَرَكَةِ وَ خَرَجَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ ( عليه السلام ) وَ جَبْرَئِيلُ جَمِيعاً مِنَ الْبِئْرِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَفِضْ عَلَيْكَ يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ طُفْ حَوْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَهَذِهِ سُقْيَا سَقَاهَا اللَّهُ وُلْدَ إِسْمَاعِيلَ فَسَارَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ شَيَّعَهُ إِسْمَاعِيلُ حَتَّى خَرَجَ مِنَ الْحَرَمِ فَذَهَبَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ رَجَعَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ إِلَى الْحَرَمِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya and Ahmad Bin Idrees, from Isa Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Ayoub, from Ali Bin Mahziyar, from Al Husayn Bin saeed, from Ali Bin Mansour, from Kulsoum in Abdul Momin Al Harrany,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Commanded Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> that he should perform Hajj and Ismail<sup>as</sup> should perform Hajj along with him<sup>as</sup> and settle him<sup>as</sup> in the sanctuary. So he<sup>as</sup> performed Hajj upon a red camel, and there wasn’t anyone with him<sup>as</sup> except for Jibraeel<sup>as</sup>. So when he<sup>as</sup> reached the Sanctuary, Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> said to him<sup>as</sup>: ‘O Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>! Both of you<sup>as</sup> should descend and bathe before entering the Sanctuary’. So they both<sup>as</sup> descended and bathed, and he<sup>as</sup> showed them<sup>as</sup> how they<sup>as</sup> should be preparing for the Ihraam. So they<sup>as</sup> did.

Then heas instructed themas to welcome with the Hajj and instructed themas with the four Talbiyya (Here I am O Allahazwj!) which the Mursil Prophetsas had exclaimed beforehand. Then heas went with themas to Al-Safa, so they encamped, and Jibraeelas stood in between themas, and faced the House (Kaaba). So heas exclaimed Takbeer, so theyas exclaimed Takbeer, and heas extolled Allahazwj and theyas extolled Allahazwj, and heas Praised and Glorified Allahazwj, so theyas Praised and Glorified Allahazwj, and heas commended upon Himazwj and theyas both did similar to that.

And Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> proceeded, and they<sup>as</sup> both went Praising upon Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic and Glorifying Him<sup>azwj</sup> until they ended up with them<sup>as</sup> to the place of the (Black) Stone. So Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> kissed the Stone, and instructed them that they should both<sup>as</sup> kiss it and circumambulate it, seven (times). Then he<sup>as</sup> stood with them<sup>as</sup> in the place of Standing Place of Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>, so he<sup>as</sup> Prayed two Cyclces, and they both<sup>as</sup> Prayed. Then he<sup>as</sup> showed them<sup>as</sup> the rituals and what they<sup>as</sup> should be doing with it.

So when they<sup>as</sup> had fulfilled their<sup>as</sup> rituals, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Commanded Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> with the leaving and Ismail<sup>as</sup> to stay alone, not having anyone with him<sup>as</sup> apart from his<sup>as</sup> mother<sup>as</sup>. So when it was the next (year), Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Permitted Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> for the Hajj and building of the Kaaba. And it was such that the Arabs used to perform Pilgrimage to it, and rather is was in ruins except that its foundations were well known.

So when the people departed, Ismail<sup>as</sup> gathered the rocks and placed them in the middle of the Kaaba. So when Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Permitted for him<sup>as</sup> regarding the building, Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> proceeded, so he<sup>as</sup> said: ‘O my<sup>as</sup> son<sup>as</sup>! Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has Commanded us<sup>as</sup> with the building of the Kaaba’, and he<sup>as</sup> uncovered from it, and there it was, one red rock. So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Revealed unto him<sup>as</sup>: “Build the construction upon it”, and, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Sent four Angels who were gathering the rock towards it.

Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> and Ismail<sup>as</sup> used to place the rocks, and the Angels were pulling them, until twelve cubits were completed, and two doors were made for it, one door to enter from, and a door to exit from, and placed steps for it and a canopy of iron upon its doors. And the Kaaba was bare, so Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> departed and he<sup>as</sup> had evened out the House, and Ismail<sup>as</sup> stayed.

So when the people came over he<sup>as</sup> saw a woman from Himeyr whose beauty astounded them. So he<sup>as</sup> asked Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic to get him<sup>as</sup> to be married to her, and she used to have a husband for her. So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Judge upon her husband with the death, and she stayed at Makkah in grief upon her husband. So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Eased that from her and Ismail<sup>as</sup> married her.

And Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> proceeded to Hajj, and the woman stayed at home and Ismail<sup>as</sup> went out to Al-Taif to acquire some provisions for his<sup>as</sup> family. So she looked at an old disheveled man. So he<sup>as</sup> asked about their state, and she informed him<sup>as</sup> with the goodness of the state. So he<sup>as</sup> asked her about him<sup>as</sup> in particular. So she informed him<sup>as</sup> with the goodness of the Religion, and he<sup>as</sup> asked her: ‘Where are you from?’ So she said, ‘A woman from Himeyr’. So Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> went and did not meet Ismail<sup>as</sup>, and Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> had written a letter, so he<sup>as</sup> said: ‘Hand it over to your husband when he<sup>as</sup> comes, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Willing’.

Ismail<sup>as</sup> came back to her, so she handed over the letter to him<sup>as</sup>. He<sup>as</sup> read it and said: ‘Do you know who this old man is?’ So she said, ‘I had seen him<sup>as</sup> as more handsome than you<sup>as</sup> in his<sup>as</sup> resemblance’. He<sup>as</sup> said: ‘That is Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>’. So she said, ‘Aah! Evil is what I was with him<sup>as</sup>’. So he<sup>as</sup> said: ‘And why is that so? Did he<sup>as</sup> look at anything from your beauty?’ She said, ‘No, but I fear that I have been deficient’. And the woman said to him<sup>as</sup>, and she was intelligent, ‘So why don’t we hand curtains upon these two doors, one curtains from over here, and one curtain from over here?’ So he<sup>as</sup> said to her: ‘Yes’.

So they made two curtains for these two (doors), the length of which were twelve cubits, to be upon the two doors. That astounded them both, so she said, ‘So why don’t I weave clothes for the Kaaba, so I could veil all of it, for these stones are dull’ So Ismail<sup>as</sup> said to her: ‘Yes’. So she hurried with regards to that and sent a message to her people for a lot of wool to be spun by them.

Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And rather, the spinning by the women occurred from that, some of them for the other, due to that. So she was quick and was aided in that, so every time she was free from a strip, so hung it. So the season (for Hajj) came, and there remained one faced from the facets of the Kaaba, so she said to Ismail<sup>as</sup>, ‘How shall we deal with this facet which is not covered by the apparel?’ So he<sup>as</sup> covered it with fibres.

So the season (for Hajj) came, and the Arabs came over upon a state which they used to come, so they looked at a matter which amazed them. So they said, ‘It is befitting for the worker of this House that one makes an offering to him’. So from then on, the offering occurred, and every tribe from the Arabs came with something, carrying it, from money, and from things other than that, until a lot of things became gathered. So they removed that fibre and completed the apparel of the House (Kaaba), and hung it upon the two doors.

And the Kaaba wasn’t with a ceiling, so Ismail<sup>as</sup> placed columns therein similar to these columns which you are seeing (today), from wood. And Ismail<sup>as</sup> roofed it with the boards and evened it with the clay. So the Arabs came from the (next) year and they entered the Kaaba and saw its construction, so they said, ‘It is befitting for the worker of this House (Kaaba) that he should be increased’. So when it was the following year they came with the offerings. Ismail<sup>as</sup> did not know how he<sup>as</sup> should be dealing with it, so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Revealed unto him<sup>as</sup> he<sup>as</sup> should sacrificed (the animals) and feed it to the Pilgrims’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And Ismail<sup>as</sup> complained to Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> of the scarcity of the water, so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Revealed unto Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> that he<sup>as</sup> should dig a well from there to occur drinking for the Pilgrims from it. So Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> descended and dug their<sup>as</sup> well, meaning Zamzam, until its water appeared. Then Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> said: ‘Descend, O Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>!’ So he<sup>as</sup> descended after Jibraeel<sup>as</sup>. So he<sup>as</sup> said; ‘O Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>! Strike in the four corners of the well and say: ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> struck in the corner which faced the House and said: ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’ So a spring sprouted. Then he<sup>as</sup> struck in the second corner and said: ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’ So a spring sprouted. Then he<sup>as</sup> struck in the third, and said: ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’ So a spring sprouted. Then he<sup>as</sup> struck in the fourth, and said: ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’ So a spring sprouted.

And Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> said to him<sup>as</sup>: ‘Drink, O Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>, and supplicate for your<sup>as</sup> son in it, for the Blessing’. And Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> and Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> came out from the well together. So he<sup>as</sup> said to him<sup>as</sup>: ‘I<sup>as</sup> leave it upon you<sup>as</sup>, O Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>, and circumambulate the House, for this here is a watering place which Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Quench the children of Ismail<sup>as</sup>’. So Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> went and Ismail<sup>as</sup> accompanied him<sup>as</sup> until he<sup>as</sup> went out from the Sanctuary. Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> went away, and Ismail<sup>as</sup> returned to the Sanctuary’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya and Ahmad ibn Idris have narrated from ‘Isa ibn Muhammad ibn abu Ayyub from Ali ibn Mahziyar from al- Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Ali ibn Mansur from Kulthum ibn ‘Abd al-Mu’min al-Harraniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, commanded Ibrahim, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, to perform Hajj and that Isma‘il also perform Hajj along with him, and then settle him in the Sacred area. They went for Hajj on a red camel and there was no one with them except Jibril. When they arrived in the sacred area, Jibril said, ‘O Ibrahim, disembark and take a shower before entering the sacred area.’ They disembarked and took a shower. He (Jibril) instructed them how to prepare for Ihram, which they followed. He (Jibril) then commanded them to pronounce the reading to commence acts of Hajj and commanded to pronounce the four Talbiyat (O Allah, here I am O Allah; here I am to obey Your command). It is the pronouncement and the recitation, which is pronounced by the messengers. He (Jibril) went with them to al-Safa’. They then disembarked there. Jibril stood between them, facing the House. He said Takbir (Allah is great beyond description) and they also said Takbir (Allah is great beyond description). He said Tahlil (no one deserves worship except Allah) and they said Tahlil. He (Jibril) said Tahmid (all praise belongs to Allah). They also did the same. He (Jibril) pronounced Tamjid (all glory belongs to Allah). They also did as he had done. He (Jibril) praised Allah. They also praised Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, as he had done. He (Jibril) then moved forward. They also did as he was doing until they reached the Stone. He (Jibril) touched it and commanded them to do the same. He (Jibril) made them to perform Tawaf (walk seven times around the Ka‘bah). He (Jibril) then made them stand at the site of the station of Ibrahim. He (Jibril) performed two Rak‘at salat (prayer). They also did as he had done. He then explained to them how to perform the rest of the acts of Hajj, and when they completed the acts of Hajj, Allah commanded Ibrahim to go back. Isma‘il stayed and there was no one with him except his mother. The next time, Allah commanded Ibrahim to perform Hajj and build the Ka‘bah. Arabs would perform Hajj also, but it (al-Ka‘bah) had fallen in pieces; however, its foundation was clearly recognizable. Isma‘il collected the falling pieces of the wall; when people moved away, he placed them inside the Ka‘bah. When Allah commanded him to build, Ibrahim came and said, ‘Son, Allah has commanded us to build al-Ka‘bah.’ They uncovered it and it was one red piece of stone only. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, then commanded them to set the foundation of al-Ka‘bah on it. Allah, the Most Majestic, the most Glorious, sent four angels who collected stones for him. Ibrahim and Isma‘il would build and the angels would bring stones to them. When completed, it was twelve yards high and they made two doors for it: one entrance and one as an exit. They made steps for it and a canopy of iron for each door. Al-Ka‘bah had no covering. Ibrahim left when the House was complete and Isma‘il stayed. When people came to him, he saw a woman from Himyar. He liked her beauty and asked Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, to give her to him in marriage. She was already married, but Allah caused him (her husband) to die and she stayed in Makkah in sadness for the death of her husband. Allah granted her solace for the death of her husband and Isma‘il married her. When Ibrahim came for Hajj, she was a perfect woman. Isma‘il went to al-Ta’if to find food for his family. She, on the other hand, saw an old man who asked her about their condition and she informed him of their good condition. He then asked her especially about him. She informed him that he was a man of good religion. He then asked her, ‘Who are you?’ She replied, ‘I am a woman from Himyar.’ Ibrahim then left without meeting Isma‘il and he wrote a letter in which he said, “You must give this letter to your husband when he comes, if Allah so wills.” Isma‘il then returned to her and she gave the letter to him. He read it and asked, ‘Do you know who this old man is?’ She replied, ‘I found him beautiful. He resembled you.’ He said, ‘He is Ibrahim.’ She then said, ‘Woe is me!’ He asked, ‘Why do you say that, did he look at your beauty?’ She replied, ‘No, but I am afraid of my shortcomings toward him.’ She said it to him, and she was an intelligent woman, ‘Allow us to hang curtains over these doors, one here and one there.’ He responded positively. They prepared two curtains, each twelve yards long and hanged them over the doors and he liked them. She then said, ‘Why do I not weave clothes for al- Ka‘bah as a covering to cover it altogether; these stones look rough.’ Isma‘il said to her, ‘That is good.’ She began to work immediately and sent to her people for a large amount of wool to spin for her. “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Woman’s spinning for each other is from that time.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘She worked quickly and sought help for this matter. As soon as she prepared a piece, she hanged it on the wall, but by the time of Hajj certain faces of the wall of al-Ka‘bah remained without cover. She then asked Isma‘il, ‘What should we do about this part that is not covered?’ They covered it with fibers of dates. At the time of Hajj, Arabs came to him and found something that was not there before, but they liked when they saw it. They said, ‘Something should be given to the keeper of this house. Each group of Arabs brought something, like leaves and other things, until a great deal was collected with them. They then removed the fibers, completed the covering for the walls and hanged the doors in place. Al-Ka‘bah did not have any roof so Isma‘il prepared pillars like the ones that you see from wood. Isma‘il made the roof with boards and leveled it with clay. Arabs came from places around it, entered al-Ka‘bah, saw the building and they said, ‘The keeper of this house should get more offerings.’ Next time they brought more offerings and Isma‘il did not know what to do about them. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, sent him revelation to slaughter them and feed them to people performing Hajj. He (the Imam) said, ‘Isma‘il then complained before Ibrahim for lack of water. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, sent revelation that said to Ibrahim to dig a well from which people performing Hajj can get water. He (Jibril) came and dug in the existing well, Zamzam, until water showed up. He (Jibril) then said, ‘O Ibrahim, climb down.’ He did as he was told to do and Jibril climbed down after him. He (Jibril) said, ‘O Ibrahim, dig in four corners of the well and say “In the name of Allah.’” He (the Imam) said, ‘Ibrahim dug in the corner near the House saying, “In the name of Allah” and a fountain gushed. He then dug in the second corner saying, “In the name of Allah” and another fountain gushed forth. He then dug in the third corner saying, “In the name of Allah,” where yet one more fountain gushed out. He thereafter dug in the fourth corner saying, “In the name of Allah” and the fourth fountain gushed forth. Jibril then said to him, “O Ibrahim, drink and pray for your offspring for blessing.” Both Ibrahim and Jibril came out of the well. He (Jibril) asked Ibrahim to leave for Tawaf around the House. This is the water that Allah gives to the offspring of Isma‘il. Ibrahim then left and Isma‘il escorted him until they were out of the sacred area. Ibrahim then left and Isma‘il returned to the sacred area.’”

translations.bn: নিশ্চয়ই আল্লাহ আজ্জা ওয়া জাল্লা ইব্রাহীমকে কাবা নির্মাণ, তার ভিত্তি উঁচু করা এবং মানুষদের মানাসিক দেখানোর আদেশ করলেন। তখন ইব্রাহীম ও ইসমাইল প্রতিদিন স্তরে স্তরে বাইতুল্লাহ নির্মাণ করলেন যতক্ষণ না হাজারে আসওয়াদের স্থানে পৌঁছলেন।
translations.de: Wahrlich, Gott, der Mächtige und Majestätische, befahl Ibrahim, die Kaaba zu bauen, ihre Fundamente zu erheben und den Menschen ihre Rituale zu zeigen. So bauten Ibrahim und Ismail das Haus jeden Tag, Schicht für Schicht, bis sie an den Ort des Schwarzen Steins gelangten.
translations.en: Indeed, God, Mighty and Majestic, commanded Ibrahim to build the Kaaba and to raise its foundations and show the people their rituals. So Ibrahim and Ismail built the House every day, course by course, until they reached the place of the Black Stone.
translations.es: Ciertamente, Dios, Poderoso y Majestuoso, ordenó a Ibrahim construir la Kaaba, elevar sus cimientos y mostrar a las personas sus rituales. Entonces Ibrahim e Ismail construyeron la Casa cada día, hilada por hilada, hasta llegar al lugar de la Piedra Negra.
translations.fa: همانا خداوند عزوجل ابراهیم را فرمان داد که کعبه را بنا کند و پایه‌هایش را بالا ببرد و مناسک حج را به مردم نشان دهد. پس ابراهیم و اسماعیل خانه را هر روز ردیف به ردیف ساختند تا اینکه به جای حجر الأسود رسیدند.
translations.fr: Certes, Dieu, Puissant et Majestueux, ordonna à Ibrahim de construire la Kaaba, d'élever ses fondations et de montrer aux gens leurs rites. Ainsi Ibrahim et Ismaïl construisirent la Maison chaque jour, assise par assise, jusqu'à parvenir à l'emplacement de la Pierre Noire.
translations.id: Sesungguhnya Allah 'Azza wa Jalla memerintahkan Ibrahim untuk membangun Ka'bah, mengangkat pondasi-pondasinya, dan memperlihatkan manasik kepada manusia. Maka Ibrahim dan Ismail membangun Baitullah setiap hari lapis demi lapis hingga sampai ke tempat Hajar Aswad.
translations.ru: Поистине, Аллах, Могущественный и Величественный, повелел Ибрахиму построить Каабу, воздвигнуть её основания и показать людям их обряды. И Ибрахим с Исмаилом строили Дом каждый день, ряд за рядом, пока не дошли до места Чёрного Камня.
translations.tr: Muhakkak ki Allah (azze ve celle), İbrahim'e Kâbe'yi inşa etmesini ve temellerini yükseltmesini ve insanlara menasiklerini göstermesini emretti. Böylece İbrahim ve İsmail Beytullah'ı her gün sıra sıra, tâ ki Hacer-ül Esved'in yerine gelene kadar inşa ettiler.
translations.ur: بے شک اللہ عزوجل نے ابراہیم کو کعبہ کی تعمیر کا حکم دیا اور یہ کہ وہ اس کی بنیادیں اٹھائیں اور لوگوں کو مناسک دکھائیں۔ پس ابراہیم اور اسماعیل نے ہر دن ایک ایک تہہ کر کے بیت اللہ تعمیر کی، یہاں تک کہ حجر اسود کی جگہ پر پہنچ گئے۔
translations.zh: 确实，至尊至大的安拉命令易卜拉欣建造克尔白，抬高其根基，并向人们展示朝觐礼仪。于是易卜拉欣和伊斯玛仪每天一层一层地建造天房，直到到达黑石的位置。

translations.bn: আবু জাফর আলাইহিস সালাম বললেন: তারপর আবু কুবাইস পর্বত ইব্রাহীম আলাইহিস সালামকে ডেকে বলল: 'আমার কাছে তোমার জন্য একটি আমানত আছে!' তখন সে তাঁকে পাথরটি দিল এবং তিনি সেটি তার স্থানে রাখলেন। তারপর ইব্রাহীম আলাইহিস সালাম মানুষদের মধ্যে হজের ঘোষণা করলেন এবং বললেন: 'হে মানুষ! আমি ইব্রাহীম, আল্লাহর বন্ধু! আল্লাহ তোমাদের এই ঘরের হজ করার আদেশ করছেন, তাই এর হজ কর!' তখন কিয়ামত পর্যন্ত যারা হজ করবে তারা সাড়া দিলেন এবং প্রথম সাড়া দেওয়ারা ইয়েমেনের অধিবাসীদের মধ্য থেকে ছিল।
translations.de: Abu Ja'far (Friede sei mit ihm) sagte: Dann rief der Berg Abu Qubays Ibrahim (Friede sei mit ihm) zu: 'Bei mir ist ein Anvertrautes für dich!' Er gab ihm den Stein, und er legte ihn an seinen Platz. Dann rief Ibrahim (Friede sei mit ihm) unter den Menschen zum Hajj auf und sagte: 'O ihr Menschen! Ich bin Ibrahim, der Freund Gottes! Gott befiehlt euch, den Hajj dieses Hauses zu verrichten — so verrichtet seinen Hajj!' Die, welche den Hajj bis zum Tag der Auferstehung verrichten werden, antworteten ihm, und die ersten, die ihm antworteten, waren aus dem Volk des Jemen.
translations.en: Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: Then Mount Abu Qubays called out to Ibrahim (peace be upon him): 'There is a trust with me for you!' So it gave him the Stone, and he placed it in its place. Then Ibrahim (peace be upon him) called out among the people for the Hajj and said: 'O people! I am Ibrahim, the Friend of God! God commands you to perform the Hajj of this House, so perform its Hajj!' So those who would perform Hajj until the Day of Resurrection answered him, and the first to answer him were from the people of Yemen.
translations.es: Abu Ja'far (la paz sea con él) dijo: Entonces el Monte Abu Qubays llamó a Ibrahim (la paz sea con él): '¡Hay un depósito para ti conmigo!' Le dio la Piedra y él la colocó en su lugar. Luego Ibrahim (la paz sea con él) proclamó el Hajj entre la gente y dijo: '¡Oh gente! Yo soy Ibrahim, el amigo íntimo de Dios. ¡Dios os ordena que hagáis el Hajj de esta Casa, así que hacedlo!' Le respondieron todos los que realizarán el Hajj hasta el Día de la Resurrección, y los primeros en responderle fueron de la gente de Yemen.
translations.fa: ابوجعفر علیه السلام فرمود: سپس کوه ابوقبیس ابراهیم علیه السلام را صدا زد: «نزد من امانتی برای تو است!» پس سنگ را به او داد و او آن را در جای خود نهاد. سپس ابراهیم علیه السلام در میان مردم به حج ندا داد و گفت: «ای مردم! من ابراهیم، خلیل خداوند هستم! خداوند به شما دستور می‌دهد که حج این خانه را به جا آورید، پس آن را حج کنید!» پس کسانی که تا روز قیامت حج می‌کنند اجابت کردند و اولین آنها از اهل یمن بودند.
translations.fr: Abu Ja'far (que la paix soit sur lui) dit : Ensuite, le Mont Abu Qubays appela Ibrahim (que la paix soit sur lui) : 'Il y a un dépôt pour toi chez moi !' Il lui donna la Pierre et il la plaça à sa place. Puis Ibrahim (que la paix soit sur lui) annonça le Hajj parmi les gens et dit : 'Ô gens ! Je suis Ibrahim, l'ami intime de Dieu ! Dieu vous ordonne de faire le Hajj de cette Maison, faites-en le Hajj !' Ceux qui feraient le Hajj jusqu'au Jour de la Résurrection lui répondirent, et les premiers à lui répondre étaient parmi les gens du Yémen.
translations.id: Abu Ja'far (as) bersabda: Kemudian Gunung Abu Qubays menyeru Ibrahim (as): 'Ada titipan untukmu padaku!' Maka memberikannya batu itu dan ia meletakkannya di tempatnya. Kemudian Ibrahim (as) mengumandangkan seruan haji di antara manusia dan berkata: 'Wahai manusia! Aku adalah Ibrahim, kekasih Allah! Allah memerintahkan kalian untuk berhaji ke rumah ini, maka berhajilah kepadanya!' Maka mereka yang akan melaksanakan haji hingga Hari Kiamat menjawabnya, dan yang pertama menjawabnya adalah dari penduduk Yaman.
translations.ru: Абу Джафар (мир ему) сказал: Затем гора Абу Кубайс воззвала к Ибрахиму (мир ему): 'У меня есть для тебя вверенное тебе!' И он дал ему Камень, и тот поместил его на своё место. Затем Ибрахим (мир ему) возгласил среди людей о Хадже и сказал: 'О люди! Я — Ибрахим, возлюбленный Аллаха! Аллах повелевает вам совершить хадж к этому Дому, так совершайте же его хадж!' И ответили ему те, кто будет совершать хадж вплоть до Дня Воскресения, и первыми, кто ответил ему, были жители Йемена.
translations.tr: Ebu Ca'fer (a.s.) buyurdu: Sonra Ebu Kubeys Dağı İbrahim'e (a.s.) seslendi: 'Sana ait bir emanet bende var!' Ve ona taşı verdi; o da onu yerine yerleştirdi. Sonra İbrahim (a.s.) insanlar arasında haccı ilan etti ve şöyle dedi: 'Ey insanlar! Ben İbrahim, Allah'ın haliliyim! Allah size bu evi haccetmenizi emrediyor, öyleyse onu haccetseniz!' Kıyamete kadar haccedecek olanlar ona cevap verdi; ilk cevap verenler Yemen halkından idi.
translations.ur: ابو جعفر علیہ السلام نے فرمایا: پھر ابو قبیس نے ابراہیم علیہ السلام کو پکارا: 'تمہارے لیے میرے پاس ایک امانت ہے!' پس اس نے انہیں پتھر دیا اور انہوں نے اسے اپنی جگہ رکھ دیا۔ پھر ابراہیم علیہ السلام نے لوگوں میں حج کا اعلان کیا اور فرمایا: 'اے لوگو! میں ابراہیم، خلیل اللہ ہوں! اللہ تمہیں حکم دیتا ہے کہ اس گھر کا حج کرو، پس اس کا حج ادا کرو!' پس قیامت تک حج کرنے والوں نے جواب دیا اور سب سے پہلے اہل یمن نے جواب دیا۔
translations.zh: 艾布·贾法尔（愿主赐他平安）说：随后，库拜斯山向易卜拉欣（愿主赐他平安）呼唤道：'我这里有一件你的托付！'于是它将石头交给他，他将其放置在了其位置上。然后易卜拉欣（愿主赐他平安）在人们中间宣告朝觐，说：'啊，人们！我是易卜拉欣，安拉的挚友！安拉命令你们朝觐这座天房，就去朝觐它吧！'于是直至复活日所有将要朝觐的人都响应了他，最先响应他的是也门人。

translations.bn: তিনি বললেন: ইব্রাহীম আলাইহিস সালাম হজ করলেন — তিনি নিজে, তাঁর পরিবার এবং তাঁর সন্তানরা। সুতরাং যে ব্যক্তি দাবি করে যে কোরবান ইসহাক ছিলেন — তাহলে এখান থেকেই তাকে কোরবান করা হয়েছিল।
translations.de: Er sagte: Ibrahim (Friede sei mit ihm) verrichtete den Hajj — er selbst, seine Familie und seine Kinder. Also, wer behauptet, dass der Geopferte Ishaq war — von genau diesem Ort aus wäre er geopfert worden.
translations.en: He said: And Ibrahim (peace be upon him) performed the Hajj — he himself, his family, and his children. So whoever claims that the one to be slaughtered was Ishaq — it would have been from this very place he was slaughtered.
translations.es: Dijo: Ibrahim (la paz sea con él) realizó el Hajj — él mismo, su familia y sus hijos. Entonces, quien afirme que el sacrificado fue Isaac, habría sido sacrificado desde este mismo lugar.
translations.fa: فرمود: ابراهیم علیه السلام خودش و خانواده‌اش و فرزندانش حج گزاردند. پس کسی که ادعا کند ذبیح اسحاق بوده، از اینجا بود که او را ذبح کرده.
translations.fr: Il dit : Ibrahim (que la paix soit sur lui) accomplit le Hajj — lui-même, sa famille et ses enfants. Donc, quiconque prétend que l'immolé était Ishaq, c'est de cet endroit même qu'il aurait été immolé.
translations.id: Ia berkata: Dan Ibrahim (as) berhaji — ia sendiri, keluarganya, dan anak-anaknya. Maka barangsiapa yang mengklaim bahwa yang disembelih adalah Ishaq, maka dari sinilah ia disembelih.
translations.ru: Он сказал: Ибрахим (мир ему) совершил хадж — сам, и его семья, и его дети. Итак, кто утверждает, что принесённый в жертву — это Исхак, значит, именно здесь он был принесён в жертву.
translations.tr: Buyurdu: İbrahim (a.s.) kendisi, ailesi ve çocukları ile hacca gitti. Kurban edilenin İshak olduğunu iddia edenlere gelince, o tam buradan kurban edilmiş olurdu.
translations.ur: آپ نے فرمایا: ابراہیم علیہ السلام نے حج کیا — خود انہوں نے، ان کے گھر والوں نے اور ان کی اولاد نے۔ پس جو کوئی یہ گمان کرے کہ ذبیح اسحاق تھے — تو یہیں سے ذبح کیا گیا۔
translations.zh: 他说：易卜拉欣（愿主赐他平安）亲自与他的家人和子嗣一同朝觐。凡声称被祭献者是伊斯哈格的人——正是从这个地方将其祭献。

translations.bn: এবং আবু বাসীর থেকে উল্লেখ করা হয়েছে যে তিনি আবু জাফর ও আবু আবদিল্লাহ আলাইহিস সালামকে উভয়কেই এটি দাবি করতে শুনেছিলেন যে সেটি ইসহাক ছিলেন, আর যুরারা দাবি করেছিলেন যে সেটি ইসমাইল ছিলেন।
translations.de: Und es wird von Abu Basir überliefert, dass er Abu Ja'far und Abu Abdillah (Friede sei mit ihm) beide behaupten hörte, dass es Ishaq war, während Zurara behauptete, dass es Ismail war.
translations.en: And it is mentioned from Abu Basir that he heard Abu Ja'far and Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) both claiming that it was Ishaq, while Zurara claimed that it was Ismail.
translations.es: Y se menciona de Abu Basir que escuchó a Abu Ja'far y a Abu Abdillah (la paz sea con él) afirmando ambos que era Isaac, mientras que Zurara afirmó que era Ismail.
translations.fa: و نقل شده از ابوبصیر که او ابوجعفر و ابوعبدالله علیه السلام را شنید که هر دو ادعا می‌کردند که او اسحاق بود، اما زراره ادعا کرد که او اسماعیل بود.
translations.fr: Et il est mentionné d'Abu Basir qu'il entendit Abu Ja'far et Abu Abdillah (que la paix soit sur lui) affirmer tous deux que c'était Ishaq, tandis que Zurara affirmait que c'était Ismaïl.
translations.id: Dan disebutkan dari Abu Basir bahwa ia mendengar Abu Ja'far dan Abu Abdillah (as) keduanya mengklaim bahwa itu adalah Ishaq, sedangkan Zurara mengklaim bahwa itu adalah Ismail.
translations.ru: И передаётся от Абу Басира, что он слышал, как Абу Джафар и Абу Абдиллах (мир ему) оба утверждали, что это был Исхак, тогда как Зурара утверждал, что это был Исмаил.
translations.tr: Ebu Basir'den rivayet edilmiştir ki, o Ebu Ca'fer ve Ebu Abdullah'ı (a.s.) bunun İshak olduğunu iddia ederken duymuş; Zurare ise bunun İsmail olduğunu iddia etmiştir.
translations.ur: اور ابو بصیر سے نقل کیا گیا ہے کہ انہوں نے ابو جعفر اور ابو عبداللہ علیہ السلام کو یہ کہتے سنا کہ وہ اسحاق تھے، جبکہ زرارہ کا کہنا تھا کہ وہ اسماعیل تھے۔
translations.zh: 并且提到：艾布·巴西尔听到艾布·贾法尔和艾布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）两人都声称那是伊斯哈格，而祖拉拉则声称那是伊斯玛仪。

diacritics_changes: Added full tashkeel to the Arabic text which was partially diacritized in the source

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ وَالْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدَوَيْهِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ جَمِيعاً عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا السَّلَامُ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَمَرَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بِبِنَاءِ الْكَعْبَةِ وَأَنْ يَرْفَعَ قَوَاعِدَهَا وَيُرِيَ النَّاسَ مَنَاسِكَهُمْ فَبَنَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَإِسْمَاعِيلُ الْبَيْتَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ سَافاً حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَى مَوْضِعِ الْحَجَرِ الْأَسْوَدِ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ فَنَادَى أَبُو قُبَيْسٍ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِنَّ لَكَ عِنْدِي وَدِيعَةً فَأَعْطَاهُ الْحَجَرَ فَوَضَعَهُ مَوْضِعَهُ ثُمَّ إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ أَذَّنَ فِي النَّاسِ بِالْحَجِّ فَقَالَ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنِّي إِبْرَاهِيمُ خَلِيلُ اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تَحُجُّوا هَذَا الْبَيْتَ فَحُجُّوهُ فَأَجَابَهُ مَنْ يَحُجُّ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ وَكَانَ أَوَّلُ مَنْ أَجَابَهُ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ قَالَ وَحَجَّ إِبْرَاهِيمُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ هُوَ وَأَهْلُهُ وَوَلَدُهُ فَمَنْ زَعَمَ أَنَّ الذَّبِيحَ هُوَ إِسْحَاقُ فَمِنْ هَاهُنَا كَانَ ذَبَحَهُ وَذُكِرَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ وَأَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ يَزْعُمَانِ أَنَّهُ إِسْحَاقُ فَأَمَّا زُرَارَةُ فَزَعَمَ أَنَّهُ إِسْمَاعِيلُ

narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Ibrahim al-Qummi (d. ~329 AH), prominent Shia hadith transmitter and author of Tafsir al-Qummi

narrators.ambiguity_note: Standard reference to Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, father of Ali ibn Ibrahim
narrators.known_identity: Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, early transmitter of hadiths from Imam al-Ridha (AS)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely al-Husayn ibn Muhammad al-Ash'ari al-Qummi, a companion of al-Kulayni
narrators.known_identity: al-Husayn ibn Muhammad al-Ash'ari al-Qummi

narrators.ambiguity_note: Less common narrator; appears in chains via al-Husayn ibn Muhammad

narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn Yahya al-Attar al-Qummi, prominent companion and teacher of al-Kulayni

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Isa al-Ash'ari al-Qummi, senior hadith transmitter
narrators.known_identity: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Isa al-Ash'ari al-Qummi
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr al-Bazanti (d. 221 AH), companion of Imams al-Ridha and al-Jawad (AS)
narrators.name_ar: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ
narrators.name_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr

narrators.known_identity: Aban ibn Uthman al-Ahmar al-Bajali, companion of Imam al-Sadiq (AS)
Uqba ibn Bashir al-Asadi, companion of Imam al-Baqir (AS)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to either the 5th Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS) or the 6th Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS); the narrator Uqba ibn Bashir was a companion of al-Baqir (AS), making al-Baqir more likely
narrators.known_identity: Likely Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam

key_phrases.phrase_en: Friend of God (Khalilullah) — title of Ibrahim

bn.الحجر الأسود: হাজারে আসওয়াদ — কাবার পূর্ব কোণে পবিত্র কালো পাথর
bn.الذبيح: যাবীহ — ইব্রাহীম যে সন্তানকে কোরবান করতে চেয়েছিলেন

de.الحجر الأسود: Der Schwarze Stein — ein heiliger Stein in der Ostecke der Kaaba
de.الذبيح: Der Geopferte — der Sohn, den Ibrahim zu opfern bereit war
de.الكعبة: Die Kaaba — die heiligste Stätte des Islam

en.أبو قبيس: Mount Abu Qubays — a mountain adjacent to Mecca, believed in Islamic tradition to have been a repository for the Black Stone
en.الحجر الأسود: The Black Stone — a sacred stone embedded in the eastern corner of the Kaaba, said to have descended from Paradise
en.الذبيح: The one to be slaughtered — the son whom Ibrahim (AS) was commanded to sacrifice; the identity (Ismail or Ishaq) is a matter of scholarly debate
en.الكعبة: The Kaaba — the cubic structure at the center of al-Masjid al-Haram in Mecca, the holiest site in Islam
en.خليل الله: Friend of God (Khalilullah) — a title of Prophet Ibrahim (AS), from Quran 4:125

es.الحجر الأسود: La Piedra Negra — piedra sagrada en el ángulo oriental de la Kaaba
es.الذبيح: El sacrificado — el hijo que Ibrahim ofreció en sacrificio
es.الكعبة: La Kaaba — el lugar más sagrado del Islam

fa.الحجر الأسود: حجر الاسود — سنگ مقدس در رکن شرقی کعبه
fa.الذبيح: ذبیح — فرزندی که ابراهیم خواست قربانی کند

fr.الحجر الأسود: La Pierre Noire — pierre sacrée dans l'angle oriental de la Kaaba
fr.الذبيح: L'immolé — le fils qu'Ibrahim offrit en sacrifice
fr.الكعبة: La Kaaba — le lieu le plus sacré de l'Islam

id.الحجر الأسود: Hajar Aswad — batu hitam suci di sudut timur Ka'bah
id.الذبيح: Dhabih — putra yang ditawarkan Ibrahim untuk dikorbankan
id.الكعبة: Ka'bah — tempat paling suci dalam Islam

ru.الحجر الأسود: Чёрный Камень — священный камень в восточном углу Каабы
ru.الذبيح: Принесённый в жертву — сын, которого Ибрахим был готов пожертвовать

tr.الحجر الأسود: Hacer-ül Esved — Kâbe'nin doğu köşesindeki kutsal taş
tr.الذبيح: Kurban — İbrahim'in kurban etmeye niyetlendiği oğlu

ur.الحجر الأسود: حجر اسود — کعبہ کے مشرقی کونے میں نصب مقدس پتھر
ur.الذبيح: ذبیح — وہ بیٹا جسے ابراہیم علیہ السلام نے قربانی کے لیے پیش کیا
ur.الكعبة: کعبہ — مکہ میں مسجد الحرام کے مرکز میں واقع مقدس عمارت

zh.الحجر الأسود: 黑石——嵌在克尔白东角的神圣石头
zh.الذبيح: 被祭献者——易卜拉欣愿意献祭的儿子
zh.الكعبة: 克尔白——伊斯兰教最神圣的圣地

seo_questions.bn: আল-কাফিতে ইব্রাহীম ও ইসমাইলের কাবা নির্মাণ এবং হাজারে আসওয়াদ সম্পর্কে কী বর্ণিত হয়েছে?
seo_questions.de: Was berichtet al-Kafi über Ibrahim und Ismails Bau der Kaaba und den Schwarzen Stein?
seo_questions.en: What does al-Kafi narrate about Ibrahim and Ismail building the Kaaba and the Black Stone?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué narra al-Kafi sobre Ibrahim e Ismail construyendo la Kaaba y la Piedra Negra?
seo_questions.fa: در الکافی درباره بنای کعبه توسط ابراهیم و اسماعیل و حجر الاسود چه روایت شده است؟
seo_questions.fr: Que rapporte al-Kafi sur la construction de la Kaaba par Ibrahim et Ismaïl et la Pierre Noire ?
seo_questions.id: Apa yang diriwayatkan al-Kafi tentang Ibrahim dan Ismail membangun Ka'bah dan Hajar Aswad?
seo_questions.ru: Что рассказывает аль-Кафи о строительстве Каабы Ибрахимом и Исмаилом и Чёрном Камне?
seo_questions.tr: el-Kâfî'de İbrahim ve İsmail'in Kâbe'yi inşa etmesi ve Hacer-ül Esved hakkında neler anlatılmaktadır?
seo_questions.ur: الکافی میں ابراہیم اور اسماعیل کے کعبہ کی تعمیر اور حجر اسود کے بارے میں کیا روایت ہے؟
seo_questions.zh: 《教法根源》中关于易卜拉欣和伊斯玛仪建造克尔白及黑石有何记载？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths narrating the story of Ibrahim and Ismail raising the foundations of the Kaaba, as referenced in Quran 2:127, with emphasis on the supplication they made during construction.

Traditions discussing the identity of the Dhabih (the son offered for sacrifice by Ibrahim), with some sources attributing it to Ishaq and others to Ismail, reflecting the classic intra-Islamic scholarly debate.
Hadiths about the primordial call to Hajj by Ibrahim, its cosmic significance, and the notion that all souls who would ever perform Hajj responded to his call before their earthly existence.

summaries.bn: এই হাদীসটি আল্লাহর নবী ইব্রাহীম আলাইহিস সালামকে কাবা নির্মাণ ও হজের আচার প্রতিষ্ঠার ঐশী আদেশের বর্ণনা করে। আবু কুবাইস পর্বত থেকে হাজারে আসওয়াদের অলৌকিকভাবে আনা, হজের জন্য ইব্রাহীমের আহ্বান এবং যবীহের পরিচয় নিয়ে আলোচনা অন্তর্ভুক্ত।
summaries.de: Dieser Hadith schildert den göttlichen Befehl an den Propheten Ibrahim (as), die Kaaba zu bauen und die Rituale des Hajj zu etablieren. Die Erzählung umfasst die wunderbare Rückholung des Schwarzen Steins vom Berg Abu Qubays, Ibrahims Ruf zum Hajj, der bis zum Jüngsten Tag hallt, und die Diskussion früher schiitischer Gelehrter über die Identität des Geopferten.
summaries.en: This hadith narrates the divine command to Prophet Ibrahim (AS) to build the Kaaba and establish the rites of Hajj. The narrative includes the miraculous retrieval of the Black Stone from Mount Abu Qubays, Ibrahim's proclamation calling humanity to pilgrimage — a call that reverberates through all time — and his personal performance of Hajj. It concludes with a scholarly discussion among early Shia authorities about the identity of the 'Dhabih' (the one offered for sacrifice) — with Imams al-Baqir and al-Sadiq reportedly claiming it was Ishaq, while Zurara reported it was Ismail, reflecting an ongoing theological debate.
summaries.es: Este hadiz narra el mandato divino al Profeta Ibrahim (AS) de construir la Kaaba y establecer los ritos del Hajj. La narración incluye la recuperación milagrosa de la Piedra Negra del Monte Abu Qubays, la proclamación de Ibrahim que resuena hasta el Juicio Final, y la discusión de las primeras autoridades chiíes sobre la identidad del Dhabih.
summaries.fa: این حدیث بیانگر فرمان الهی به حضرت ابراهیم علیه السلام برای بنای کعبه و برپایی مناسک حج است. روایت شامل آوردن معجزه‌آسای حجر الاسود از کوه ابوقبیس، ندای ابراهیم برای حج که تا قیامت طنین‌انداز است، و بحث علمی درباره هویت ذبیح می‌شود.
summaries.fr: Ce hadith relate le commandement divin au Prophète Ibrahim (as) de construire la Kaaba et d'établir les rites du Hajj. La narration inclut la récupération miraculeuse de la Pierre Noire depuis le Mont Abu Qubays, la proclamation d'Ibrahim qui résonne jusqu'au Jour du Jugement, et la discussion des premières autorités chiites sur l'identité du Dhabih.
summaries.id: Hadis ini menceritakan perintah Ilahi kepada Nabi Ibrahim (as) untuk membangun Ka'bah dan menetapkan ritual haji. Narasi mencakup pengambilan Hajar Aswad secara mukjizat dari Gunung Abu Qubays, seruan Ibrahim untuk berhaji yang bergema hingga Hari Kiamat, dan diskusi para ulama Syiah awal tentang identitas 'Dhabih'.
summaries.ru: Этот хадис повествует о Божественном повелении пророку Ибрахиму (мир ему) построить Каабу и установить обряды хаджа. В рассказ входит чудесное возвращение Чёрного Камня с горы Абу Кубайс, призыв Ибрахима к хаджу, эхо которого звучит до Судного Дня, и дискуссия ранних шиитских учёных об идентичности Дабиха.
summaries.tr: Bu hadis, Hz. İbrahim'e (as) Kâbe'yi inşa etmesini ve hac ibadetini tesis etmesini emreden ilahi buyruğu anlatır. Rivayete göre Hacer-ül Esved, Ebu Kubeys Dağı'ndan mucizevi biçimde getirilmiş; İbrahim'in kıyamete kadar yankılanacak olan hacca çağrısı ve kurban edilenin kimliğine dair erken dönem Şia alimlerinin tartışması da ele alınmaktadır.
summaries.ur: یہ حدیث حضرت ابراہیم علیہ السلام کو کعبہ کی تعمیر اور حج کے مناسک قائم کرنے کے الہی حکم کا بیان کرتی ہے۔ حجر اسود کا کوہِ ابو قبیس سے معجزانہ طور پر لایا جانا، حج کے لیے ابراہیم علیہ السلام کی پکار — جو قیامت تک گونجتی رہے گی — اور ذبیح کی شناخت کے بارے میں علماء کے اختلاف کا بیان شامل ہے۔
summaries.zh: 这段圣训叙述了安拉命令先知易卜拉欣（愿主赐他平安）建造克尔白并确立朝觐礼仪的故事。内容包括黑石从库拜斯山奇迹般被取回、易卜拉欣响彻末日的朝觐呼唤，以及早期什叶学者关于被祭献者身份的讨论。

translation.fa: پس حج آن را به جا آورید
translation.ur: پس اس کا حج ادا کرو

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/37)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَمَرَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بِبِنَاءِ الْكَعْبَةِ وَ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ قَوَاعِدَهَا وَ يُرِيَ النَّاسَ مَنَاسِكَهُمْ فَبَنَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ الْبَيْتَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ سَافاً حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَى مَوْضِعِ الْحَجَرِ الْأَسْوَدِ

قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) فَنَادَى أَبُو قُبَيْسٍ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ لَكَ عِنْدِي وَدِيعَةً فَأَعْطَاهُ الْحَجَرَ فَوَضَعَهُ مَوْضِعَهُ ثُمَّ إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) أَذَّنَ فِي النَّاسِ بِالْحَجِّ فَقَالَ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنِّي إِبْرَاهِيمُ خَلِيلُ اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تَحُجُّوا هَذَا الْبَيْتَ فَحُجُّوهُ فَأَجَابَهُ مَنْ يَحُجُّ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ كَانَ أَوَّلُ مَنْ أَجَابَهُ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ
قَالَ وَ حَجَّ إِبْرَاهِيمُ ( عليه السلام ) هُوَ وَ أَهْلُهُ وَ وَلَدُهُ فَمَنْ زَعَمَ أَنَّ الذَّبِيحَ هُوَ إِسْحَاقُ فَمَنْ هَاهُنَا كَانَ ذَبَحَهُ .
وَ ذُكِرَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَزْعُمَانِ أَنَّهُ إِسْحَاقُ فَأَمَّا زُرَارَةُ فَزَعَمَ أَنَّهُ إِسْمَاعِيلُ.

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Abduwayh Bin Aamir, and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, altogether from Ahmad Bni Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Aban Bin usman, from Uqba Bin Basheer,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) having said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Commanded Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> with the building of the House (Kaaba), and that he<sup>as</sup> should raise its foundations, and show the people their rituals (to be performed). Therefore, Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> and Ismail<sup>as</sup> built the House (Kaaba) every day, endeavouring, until they ended up to the place of the (Black) Stone’.

Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> called out on (Mount) Abu Qubays: ‘There is an entrustment with me<sup>as</sup> for you!’ So he<sup>as</sup> gave it the (Black) Stone, and he<sup>as</sup> placed it in its place. Then Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> called out among the people with the Hajj, so he<sup>as</sup> said: ‘O you people! I<sup>as</sup> am Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>, the Friend of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Commands you all that you should be performing the Hajj of this House (Kaaba)! So perform its Hajj!’ So they answered, the ones who would be performing Hajj, up to the Day of Judgement, and the first one who answered him<sup>as</sup> were from the people of Al-Yemen’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> performed Hajj, him<sup>as</sup> and his<sup>as</sup> wife and his children. So the one who claims that the slaughtered one, he was Is’haq, so it is from over here he was slaughtered’.

And it is mentioned from Abu Baseer that he heard Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> and Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> both claiming that it was Is’haq, and as for Zurara, so he claimes that it was Ismail<sup>as</sup>.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and al-Husayn ibn Muhammad have narrated from ‘ Abdawayh ibn ‘Amir and Muhammad ibn Yahya from Ahmad ibn Muhammad all from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Nasr from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Uqbah ibn Bashir who has said the following: “One of the two Imam, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said that Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, commanded Ibrahim to build al-Ka‘bah, to raise its foundations and show people how to perform the acts of their hajj. Ibrahim and Isma‘il built the House. They did it by doing a certain amount every day until they ended at the place where there is the Black Stone. Abu Ja’far has said that abu Qubays (hill), then called Ibrahim saying, ‘There is something in trust for you with me.’ It gave the Black Stone to him and he placed it in its place. Ibrahim then called people for Hajj saying, ‘O people, I am Ibrahim, the friend of Allah, and Allah commands you to perform Hajj of this House so you must perform Hajj.’ Thus, all those who up to the Day of Judgment perform Hajj answered his call. The first one who answered his call was from Yemen. He (the Imam) said, ‘Ibrahim, his family and children performed Hajj. Those who think that the person to be slaughtered was Ishaq they do so because of this.”’ It is said that abu Basir had heard this Hadith from abu Ja’far and abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhim al-Salam. These two people think that Ishaq was the one but Zurarah thinks that he was Isma‘il.

translations.bn: আবুল হাসান (আলাইহিস সালাম) — অর্থাৎ আর-রিযা — আল-হাসান ইবনুল জাহমকে বললেন: 'তোমাদের কাছে আস-সাকীনা কী?' তিনি বললেন: 'আমি জানি না, আমি আপনার জন্য উৎসর্গিত হই, এটা কী?' তিনি (আলাইহিস সালাম) বললেন: 'জান্নাত থেকে বের হওয়া একটি সুগন্ধি বায়ু, যার আকৃতি মানুষের মুখমণ্ডলের মতো। এটি নবীদের সাথে থাকে, আর এটিই সেটা যা ইব্রাহীম (আলাইহিস সালাম)-এর উপর অবতীর্ণ হয়েছিল যখন তিনি কাবা নির্মাণ করেছিলেন — এটি এভাবে এভাবে পথ দেখাতে লাগল এবং তিনি এর উপর ভিত্তি নির্মাণ করলেন।'
translations.de: Abu al-Hasan (Friede sei mit ihm) — das heißt al-Ridha — sagte zu al-Hasan ibn al-Jahm: 'Welche Sache ist al-Sakina eurer Meinung nach?' Er sagte: 'Ich weiß es nicht, möge ich dein Lösegeld sein, und was ist es?' Er (Friede sei mit ihm) sagte: 'Ein wohlriechender Wind, der aus dem Paradies kommt, mit einer Gestalt wie das Gesicht eines Menschen. Er begleitet die Propheten, und er ist derjenige, der auf Ibrahim (Friede sei mit ihm) herabkam, als er die Kaaba baute — er begann so und so zu führen, und er baute das Fundament darauf.'
translations.en: Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) — meaning al-Ridha — said to al-Hasan ibn al-Jahm: 'Which thing is al-Sakina in your view?' He said: 'I do not know, may I be your ransom, and what is it?' He (peace be upon him) said: 'A fragrant wind that comes out from Paradise, possessing a form like the face of a human being. It accompanies the Prophets, and it is that which descended upon Ibrahim (peace be upon him) when he built the Kaaba — it began guiding thus and thus, and so he built the foundation upon it.'
translations.es: Abu al-Hasan (paz sea con él) — es decir, al-Ridha — dijo a al-Hasan ibn al-Jahm: '¿Cuál cosa es al-Sakina en vuestra opinión?' Él dijo: 'No lo sé, que sea yo tu rescate, ¿y qué es eso?' Él (paz sea con él) dijo: 'Un viento fragante que sale del Paraíso, poseyendo una forma como la del rostro de un ser humano. Acompaña a los Profetas, y es aquella que descendió sobre Ibrahim (paz sea con él) cuando construyó la Kaaba — comenzó a guiar así y asá, y él construyó los cimientos sobre ella.'
translations.fa: ابوالحسن علیه السلام — یعنی الرضا — به حسن بن الجهم گفت: «نزد شما السکینه چه چیزی است؟» او گفت: «نمی‌دانم، فدایت شوم، آن چیست؟» آن حضرت فرمود: «بادی خوش‌بو که از بهشت بیرون می‌آید، دارای صورتی مانند چهره‌ی انسان. با انبیاء همراه است و همان است که بر ابراهیم علیه السلام نازل شد هنگامی که کعبه را بنا کرد — شروع کرد به این‌چنین و آن‌چنان گرفتن و او پایه را بر آن بنا نهاد.»
translations.fr: Abu al-Hasan (paix sur lui) — à savoir al-Ridha — dit à al-Hasan ibn al-Jahm : « Quelle chose est al-Sakina selon vous ? » Il dit : « Je ne sais pas, que je sois ton rançon, et qu'est-ce que c'est ? » Il (paix sur lui) dit : « Un vent parfumé qui sort du Paradis, possédant une forme semblable au visage d'un être humain. Il accompagne les Prophètes, et c'est lui qui descendit sur Ibrahim (paix sur lui) lorsqu'il construisit la Kaaba — il se mit à guider ainsi et ainsi, et il bâtit les fondations sur lui. »
translations.id: Abu al-Hasan (alayhissalam) — yaitu al-Ridha — berkata kepada al-Hasan ibn al-Jahm: 'Apa yang kamu pahami tentang al-Sakinah?' Dia berkata: 'Saya tidak tahu, semoga saya menjadi tebusanmu, dan apa itu?' Beliau (alayhissalam) bersabda: 'Angin harum yang keluar dari surga, memiliki bentuk seperti wajah manusia. Ia menyertai para Nabi, dan itulah yang turun kepada Ibrahim (alayhissalam) ketika beliau membangun Ka'bah — ia mulai memandu begini dan begitu, lalu beliau membangun fondasi di atasnya.'
translations.ru: Абу аль-Хасан (мир ему) — то есть ар-Рида — сказал аль-Хасану ибн аль-Джахму: «Какова ас-Сакина в вашем понимании?» Он сказал: «Я не знаю, пусть я стану твоим выкупом, и что же это такое?» Он (мир ему) сказал: «Благовонный ветер, исходящий из Рая, имеющий облик, подобный лицу человека. Он сопровождает Пророков, и именно он спустился на Ибрахима (мир ему), когда тот строил Каабу — он начал направлять так и эдак, и тот построил основание на нём.»
translations.tr: Ebu'l-Hasan (aleyhisselam) — yani er-Rıza — el-Hasen ibn el-Cahm'a dedi ki: 'Sizin yanınızda es-Sekine nedir?' O dedi ki: 'Bilmiyorum, sana feda olayım, nedir o?' O (aleyhisselam) dedi: 'Cennetten çıkan hoş kokulu bir rüzgardır, insan yüzü gibi bir şekli vardır. Peygamberlerle birlikte bulunur ve Kabe'yi inşa ederken İbrahim'e (aleyhisselam) inen odur — böyle böyle yol göstermeye başladı ve temeli onun üzerine bina etti.'
translations.ur: ابو الحسن علیہ السلام — یعنی الرضا — نے حسن بن الجہم سے فرمایا: 'تمہارے نزدیک السکینہ کیا چیز ہے؟' اس نے کہا: 'میں نہیں جانتا، میں آپ پر قربان، یہ کیا ہے؟' آپ علیہ السلام نے فرمایا: 'ایک خوشبودار ہوا جو جنت سے نکلتی ہے، اس کی صورت انسانی چہرے جیسی ہے۔ یہ انبیاء کے ساتھ رہتی ہے، اور یہی وہ ہے جو ابراہیم علیہ السلام پر نازل ہوئی جب انہوں نے کعبہ بنایا — اس نے ایسے اور ایسے رہنمائی کی اور آپ نے بنیاد اس پر رکھی۔'
translations.zh: 阿布尔-哈桑（愿主赐他平安）——即里达——对哈桑·伊本·贾赫姆说："在你们看来，萨基纳是什么？"他说："我不知道，愿我为您献身，它是什么？"他（愿主赐他平安）说："这是一阵从天堂散发出来的芬芳之风，具有人类面容般的形态。它伴随众先知，就是它降临于易卜拉欣（愿主赐他平安）当他建造克尔白之时——它开始这般那般引导，于是他在其上奠定基础。"

translations.bn: [দ্বিতীয় সনদ: আলী ইবনু ইব্রাহীম, তাঁর পিতা থেকে, আলী ইবনু আসবাত থেকে]
translations.de: [Zweite Kette: Ali ibn Ibrahim, von seinem Vater, von Ali ibn Asbat]
translations.en: [Second chain: Ali ibn Ibrahim, from his father, from Ali ibn Asbat]
translations.es: [Segunda cadena: Ali ibn Ibrahim, de su padre, de Ali ibn Asbat]
translations.fa: [سند دوم: علی بن ابراهیم، از پدرش، از علی بن اسباط]
translations.fr: [Deuxième chaîne : Ali ibn Ibrahim, de son père, d'Ali ibn Asbat]
translations.id: [Isnad kedua: Ali ibn Ibrahim, dari ayahnya, dari Ali ibn Asbat]
translations.ru: [Второй иснад: Али ибн Ибрахим, от своего отца, от Али ибн Асбата]
translations.tr: [İkinci isnad: Ali ibn İbrahim, babasından, Ali ibn Esbât'tan]
translations.ur: [دوسری سند: علی بن ابراہیم، اپنے والد سے، علی بن اسباط سے]
translations.zh: [第二传述链：阿里·伊本·易卜拉欣，从其父亲，从阿里·伊本·阿斯巴特]

translations.bn: তিনি বললেন: 'আমি আবুল হাসান (আলাইহিস সালাম)-কে আস-সাকীনা সম্পর্কে জিজ্ঞেস করলাম এবং তিনি এর মতো উল্লেখ করলেন।'
translations.de: Er sagte: 'Ich fragte Abu al-Hasan (Friede sei mit ihm) über die Sakina, und er erwähnte Ähnliches.'
translations.en: He said: 'I asked Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) about al-Sakina, and he mentioned similar to it.'
translations.es: Él dijo: 'Pregunté a Abu al-Hasan (paz sea con él) sobre al-Sakina, y mencionó algo similar.'
translations.fa: او گفت: «از ابوالحسن علیه السلام درباره السکینه پرسیدم و او مانند آن را ذکر کرد.»
translations.fr: Il dit : « J'ai demandé à Abu al-Hasan (paix sur lui) au sujet de al-Sakina, et il mentionna quelque chose de semblable. »
translations.id: Dia berkata: 'Saya bertanya kepada Abu al-Hasan (alayhissalam) tentang al-Sakinah, dan beliau menyebutkan yang serupa.'
translations.ru: Он сказал: «Я спросил Абу аль-Хасана (мир ему) об ас-Сакина, и он упомянул подобное.»
translations.tr: O dedi: 'Ebu'l-Hasan'a (aleyhisselam) es-Sekine hakkında sordum, o da benzerini anlattı.'
translations.ur: اس نے کہا: 'میں نے ابو الحسن علیہ السلام سے السکینہ کے بارے میں پوچھا تو آپ نے اس جیسا ذکر فرمایا۔'
translations.zh: 他说："我向阿布尔-哈桑（愿主赐他平安）询问萨基纳，他提到了相似的内容。"

Removed parenthetical honorific markup ( ) and integrated into running text
